---
title: "VIGIAR Conventions"
author: "Ryan Santos"
date: "2026-06-20"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{VIGIAR Conventions}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>", eval = FALSE)
```

## Principles

`vigiar` standardizes raw VIGIAR data into stable R-friendly structures:

1. Column names use `snake_case`.
2. Municipality codes use the 6-digit IBGE code internally.
3. The RJ registry also stores the official 7-digit IBGE code.
4. Processed objects preserve source metadata.
5. Validation functions warn or diagnose; they do not silently repair science.

## Municipality Codes

The package standard is the 6-digit IBGE municipality code. Examples:

| Input | Internal code |
| --- | --- |
| `330455` | `330455` |
| `3304557` | `330455` |
| `" 330455 "` | `330455` |

Values with non-numeric characters or unsafe lengths become `NA`.

## PM2.5 Columns

| Raw name | Standard name | Type | Unit |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `muni`, `ID_MUNI` | `cod_municipio` | integer | 6-digit IBGE code |
| `UF` | `sigla_uf` | character | state abbreviation |
| `Media_pm25` | `pm25_media_anual` | numeric | ug/m3 |
| `pm25` | `pm25_media` | numeric | ug/m3 |
| `t_dias` | `pm25_media_periodo` | numeric | ug/m3 |
| `n_dias` | `n_dias_criticos` | integer | days |
| `n_dias_conama` | `n_dias_criticos_conama` | integer | days |
| `LAT`, `LON` | `latitude`, `longitude` | numeric | decimal degrees |
| `Categoria_pm25` | `categoria_oms` | character | category |
| `Categoria_pm25_conama` | `categoria_conama` | character | category |

## Health Indicator Columns

| Raw name | Standard name | Type |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Indicador` | `indicador` | character |
| `n` | `populacao_exposta` | numeric |
| `est` | `estimativa` | numeric |
| `low` | `ic_inferior` | numeric |
| `high` | `ic_superior` | numeric |
| `desfecho` | `desfecho` | character |
| `q1`, `q2`, `q3` | `quartil_1`, `quartil_2`, `quartil_3` | numeric |

## Scientific Limits

- VIGIAR data are aggregated by geography and time.
- PM2.5 may be estimated or modelled rather than directly measured.
- Population exposure uses demographic projections or aggregate estimates.
- Health indicators are epidemiologic estimates with uncertainty.
- Municipality of residence and municipality of exposure may differ.
